definition.fr: Les Quatre Nobles Vérités forment le cœur de l'enseignement que <ph id='buddha'>le Bouddha</ph> délivre dès son premier sermon : il y a la <c id='dukkha'>souffrance</c>, elle a une origine, la <c id='tanha'>soif</c>, cette origine peut cesser, le <c id='nirvana'>nirvāṇa</c>, et il existe un chemin qui y mène, <c id='eightfold-path'>l'Octuple Sentier</c>. Leur structure est celle d'un diagnostic médical : un mal, sa cause, son pronostic, son traitement. C'est moins un dogme à croire qu'un cadre à vérifier par l'expérience.
definition.en: The Four Noble Truths form the heart of the teaching <ph id='buddha'>the Buddha</ph> delivers in his very first sermon: there is <c id='dukkha'>suffering</c>, it has an origin, <c id='tanha'>craving</c>, that origin can cease, <c id='nirvana'>nirvāṇa</c>, and there is a path that leads there, the <c id='eightfold-path'>Noble Eightfold Path</c>. Their structure is that of a medical diagnosis: an ailment, its cause, its prognosis, its treatment. They are less a dogma to be believed than a framework to be tested in experience.
